level_signals: Base salary range $176K-$220K in San Francisco
Expected to operate autonomously at highest level
'Eventually spin out to take on full ownership of a given role, team, or opportunity'
4+ years of experience cited as a nice-to-have

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: The ability to run on your own, operating at the highest level in a rapidly changing industry during a period of hypergrowth
A penchant for digging deeply into the data yourself, thinking from first principles, and iterating quickly to deliver results
A strong orientation toward outcomes, and a history of being scrappy when it counts
Low ego - you will be joining a team of truly exceptional generalist athletes, there is no room for ego
An easygoing interpersonal style that you bring to bear in everything from deep technical conversations with engineers to polished executive level comms
Proven ability to build high trust relationships with a wide range of people across the organization
A history of developing lightweight processes and getting them adopted
Experience in product or project management, taking projects from conception to launch
Excellent qualitative and quantitative analytical skills

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of experience in a highly strategic, analytical and operational role within a rapidly growing company
2+ years consulting (McKinsey, Bain, or BCG preferred), investment banking, private equity, or hedge fund experience
Experience with SQL or a willingness/aptitude to learn

scope.surface: Cross-functional BizOps & Strategy initiatives spanning product, engineering, sales, marketing, and core operations
scope.business_model: B2B AI data and full-stack technology platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Successful resolution of ambiguous strategic/operational problems
Adoption of new processes and systems
core_responsibilities: Take on Scale's difficult-but-ambiguous problems, breaking them down in a structured way and driving them to successful outcomes
Dive deeply into our operations and data - you will become an expert on how Scale operates, and develop a deep understanding of the AI industry
Build high trust relationships across the organization to partner with and influence outcomes beyond what you directly touch
Have full ownership of your work and outcome
Partner with, influence, and support the Scale executive team as we tackle the most difficult decisions in the business
Help incubate, seed, and scale new teams
Design, manage, and iterate on the operational and analytical systems and processes that make Scale hum
Eventually spin out to take on full ownership of a given role, team, or opportunity in Scale

compensation.equity_form: equity grant subject to Board of Director approval

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($176K-$220K base) in San Francisco
Equity mentioned as part of compensation package
Clear articulation of team mission and operating model
Explicit career growth path: 'eventually spin out to take on full ownership of a given role, team, or opportunity'
Broad scope and high-impact exposure to executive team and mission-critical decisions
red_flags: Most requirements listed under 'Ideally you would have' which could imply flexibility, but language is effectively unconditional — treated as hard requirements; some ambiguity remains
No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization
Location type not explicitly stated as remote or hybrid; salary range only listed for San Francisco suggesting onsite expectation
90-day waiting period policy before reconsidering candidates for the same role may be a concern for repeat applicants

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and role expectations)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.type (inferred as onsite from salary range listed only for San Francisco; no remote or hybrid language stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false; no remote option mentioned)
ambiguities: All primary qualifications are listed under 'Ideally you would have' rather than a 'Required' section, which could imply they are aspirational. However, the language is unconditional and the JD framing treats them as baseline expectations; classified as hard requirements with this note.
Nice-to-haves section lists '4+ years of experience' which typically signals a minimum bar; classified as soft per the 'Nice to haves' header but may function as a de facto screen.
